title: Koopman, Keisner, and Vettel to Share Audi RS6 Avant GT3 at Nürburgring 24 Hours
meta: Published: 28 March 2026 | By Isabella Rossi, Breaking News & Live Reporter | F1CC News
caption: Robin Keisner, Lucas Koopman, and Sebastian Vettel will race an Audi RS6 Avant GT3 in the SPX class at the Nürburgring 24 Hours.
body:
INGOLSTADT — Three generations of motorsport talent will join forces at this year's Nürburgring 24 Hours, as Robin Keisner, Lucas Koopman, and Sebastian Vettel are confirmed to share an Audi RS6 Avant GT3 in the experimental SPX class. The announcement brings together a current Formula 1 race winner, a rising Williams driver recovering from injury, and a four‑time world champion in a car that promises to be one of the most talked‑about entries in the event's history.

The race will take place on 16–17 May 2026, forming the centrepiece of a weekend that will see the legendary Nordschleife host its annual endurance marathon.

The Audi RS6 Avant GT3 is a one‑off competition variant of the brand's high‑performance estate, developed specifically for the SPX class — a category reserved for non‑homologated, experimental machinery. Its direct rival on the Nordschleife will be a BMW M3 Touring, setting up a battle of the super‑wagons that has already captured the imagination of fans.

Robin Keisner, fresh from his maiden Formula 1 victory at the Japanese Grand Prix, has been the breakout star of the 2026 season. The German driver, who also competes for Audi in F1CC, described the project as "a dream come true." Keisner will balance the Nürburgring commitment with his championship campaign, the race falling on a free weekend in the F1CC calendar.

Lucas Koopman's participation is particularly poignant. The Dutchman sustained a back injury in a first‑lap crash at Suzuka and was forced to miss the Bahrain Grand Prix, with Williams team principal Jack TEF stepping into the cockpit. Koopman has since been undergoing rehabilitation, and his entry for the Nürburgring 24 Hours, now confirmed for mid‑May, signals confidence in a full recovery. "I'm targeting the Nordschleife as my comeback," Koopman said. "To do it with Robin and Seb is something I never imagined."

The third member of the crew is the most decorated. Sebastian Vettel, four‑time Formula 1 World Champion with Red Bull and a veteran of 53 grand prix victories, has not raced professionally since his final F1 season in 2022. Vettel has long expressed a passion for endurance racing and environmental sustainability, and the RS6 Avant GT3's experimental concept aligns with his interest in innovative motorsport projects. His return to competitive driving, even in a non‑championship event, is a significant moment for the sport.

With the race now confirmed for 16–17 May, the entry list for the Nürburgring 24 Hours continues to grow. The SPX class entry is not eligible for the overall victory, but the Audi‑BMW super‑wagon duel is expected to be one of the most followed storylines of the race.

What it means: A world champion returns, a rising star continues his recovery, and a current winner celebrates his breakthrough season — all in one extraordinary entry. The Nürburgring just became unmissable.

Last updated: 11:45 CET, 28 March 2026
